behavior_summary: When the gargoyle NPC/object is examined (the U8P_GARGOYLE::look process triggers), the script emits a single short bark: "gargoyle ".
After emitting the bark the process performs a suspend/terminate step (inferred), so the U8P_GARGOYLE::look interaction ends or pauses immediately after the message.
There is no branching, menu interaction, global-flag reading/writing, or external calls—this is a minimal descriptive handler.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GARGOYLE::look is a single linear process: bark then suspend (no conditional branches or calls).
The process is registered as a U8P_GARGOYLE::look/examine handler (process type 0x0208) and therefore runs in response to player examine/inspect actions.
open_questions: Does the inferred suspend correspond to the engine's suspend/stop for U8P_GARGOYLE::look processes or is it an explicit yield that allows other processing? (confidence: low)
Is the trailing space in the bark string "gargoyle " significant (placeholder for punctuation or concatenation) or an artifact of extraction? (confidence: low)
Could this handler also be invoked by fast-area entry for a nearby trigger, or is it strictly the player-initiated U8P_GARGOYLE::look/examine action? (confidence: medium)

main_function.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines or 'looks at' the gargoyle; display a one-line bark and then end/pause the U8P_GARGOYLE::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine's U8P_GARGOYLE::look/examine event for this NPC/object (processType 0x0208).
In-game: invoked when a player inspects or targets the gargoyle to 'U8P_GARGOYLE::look' at it.

function_map.name: U8P_GARGOYLE::look
function_map.role: examine/description handler (emits a single bark and suspends)
